name: ai_incidents_20
description: 20 AI incident news articles with ground truth annotations. Values aligned with KI2 schema vocabulary.

incidents.title: OpenClaw AI Agent Exposes Widespread Security Vulnerabilities and Data Leaks
incidents.article_text: OpenClaw, an open-source agentic AI assistant, has rapidly gained popularity but triggered major security incidents. Over 1,800 exposed instances leaked sensitive data, including API keys and credentials, due to inadequate security controls. The AI's autonomous operation bypassed traditional enterprise defenses, leading to significant data breaches and property harm.

event.description: OpenClaw AI assistant exposed 1,800+ instances leaking API keys and credentials

incidents.title: Amazon Reports Massive Child Sexual Abuse Material in AI Training Data, Withholds Source Details
incidents.article_text: Amazon discovered hundreds of thousands of child sexual abuse images in externally sourced data intended for AI model training. Although the material was removed and reported to the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children, Amazon did not provide source details, hindering law enforcement efforts to identify perpetrators and protect victims.

event.description: Amazon found hundreds of thousands of child abuse images in AI training data

organizations.name: National Center for Missing & Exploited Children

incidents.title: Open-Source AI Models Exploited for Criminal Activities Due to Security Flaws
incidents.article_text: Researchers from SentinelOne and Censys found that thousands of open-source large language models, including Meta's Llama and Google DeepMind's Gemma, are being exploited by hackers for criminal activities such as scams, phishing, disinformation, and child sexual abuse material, due to removed safety features and lack of platform security.

event.description: Open-source LLMs exploited for scams, phishing, disinformation, and CSAM

incidents.title: Dow Chemical Cuts 4,500 Jobs Citing AI-Driven Restructuring
incidents.article_text: Dow Inc. announced it will cut about 4,500 jobs globally, representing roughly 12-15% of its workforce, as part of a major restructuring initiative centered on expanding artificial intelligence and automation. The company cites AI-driven operational efficiencies as a key factor behind the workforce reduction, resulting in significant economic and social impact.

event.description: Dow Inc. cuts 4,500 jobs due to AI-driven restructuring

incidents.title: AI Toy Company Exposes 50,000 Children's Chat Logs Due to Security Flaw
incidents.article_text: Security researchers discovered that Bondu, an AI toy company, left over 50,000 children's chat logs and personal data exposed via an unsecured web portal. Anyone with a Gmail account could access sensitive conversations and personal details, resulting in a major privacy breach before the company closed the vulnerability.

event.description: AI toy company exposed 50,000+ children's chat logs via unsecured portal

incidents.title: Clawdbot AI Agent Causes Financial Losses and Privacy Risks in Zero-Employee Companies
incidents.article_text: Clawdbot, a locally deployed AI agent enabling fully automated 'zero-employee' companies, has rapidly gained popularity but triggered incidents of financial loss and privacy breaches due to high system privileges and security vulnerabilities. Users reported stolen funds and data leaks, highlighting significant risks despite the technology's productivity benefits.

event.description: Clawdbot AI agent caused financial losses and privacy breaches in zero-employee companies

incidents.title: Waymo Robotaxi Strikes Child Near California School, Prompting Federal Investigation
incidents.article_text: A Waymo self-driving vehicle, operating without a safety driver, struck a child near an elementary school in Santa Monica, California. The AI system detected the child and braked, but still caused minor injuries. The incident has triggered a federal investigation into the safety of autonomous vehicles.

event.description: Waymo robotaxi struck a child near elementary school causing minor injuries

incidents.title: Ex-Google Engineer Convicted of Stealing AI Trade Secrets for Chinese Companies
incidents.article_text: Linwei Ding, a former Google software engineer, was convicted in San Francisco of stealing thousands of pages of confidential AI hardware and software trade secrets to benefit Chinese companies. The stolen information included sensitive details about Google's AI supercomputing infrastructure, leading to charges of economic espionage and trade secret theft.

event.description: Ex-Google engineer convicted of stealing AI trade secrets for Chinese companies

incidents.title: MS NOW Admits to Airing AI-Enhanced Image of Alex Pretti, Prompting Misinformation Concerns
incidents.article_text: MS NOW (formerly MSNBC) broadcast and published an AI-enhanced image of Alex Pretti, who was fatally shot by federal agents in Minneapolis. The altered image, which made Pretti appear more attractive, was used in news segments and online without disclosure, leading to public misinformation and criticism before a quiet correction was issued.

event.description: MS NOW aired AI-enhanced image of shooting victim without disclosure

incidents.title: ADL Finds xAI's Grok Chatbot Fails to Detect and Counters Antisemitism, Amplifies Harmful Content
incidents.article_text: The Anti-Defamation League's AI Index found that xAI's Grok chatbot performed worst among six leading AI models at detecting and countering antisemitic and extremist content, sometimes generating or failing to reject harmful narratives. The report highlights realized harms, including Grok's facilitation of antisemitic rhetoric and deepfake images.

event.description: xAI's Grok chatbot failed to detect antisemitism and amplified harmful content

incidents.title: Music Publishers Sue Anthropic for $3 Billion Over AI Training on Pirated Works
incidents.article_text: Universal Music Group, Concord Music Group, and others filed a lawsuit in the U.S. against Anthropic, alleging its Claude AI models were trained on over 20,000 copyrighted songs illegally downloaded from pirate sites. The publishers seek over $3 billion in damages for copyright infringement and DMCA violations.

event.description: Music publishers sue Anthropic for $3B over AI training on pirated songs

incidents.title: Waymo and Tesla Robotaxis Involved in Safety Incidents Raise Concerns Over Autonomous Vehicle AI
incidents.article_text: A Waymo autonomous vehicle in San Francisco struck a child, causing minor injuries and raising concerns about AI's ability to handle unpredictable pedestrian behavior. Separately, Tesla's robotaxis in Texas were found to crash nine times more often than human-driven cars, highlighting significant safety and reliability issues with current AI-driven vehicles.

event.event_location: San Francisco and Texas, United States
event.description: Waymo struck child; Tesla robotaxis crash 9x more often than human drivers

incidents.title: Apple and Google Criticized for Hosting AI Nudify Apps Enabling Non-Consensual Deepfakes
incidents.article_text: Apple and Google have been criticized for hosting dozens of AI-powered 'nudify' apps on their app stores, which generate non-consensual sexualized deepfake images. Despite policies against such content, these apps have been downloaded hundreds of millions of times, causing significant privacy violations and harm to individuals.

event.description: Apple and Google hosting AI nudify apps enabling non-consensual deepfakes

incidents.title: xAI's Grok Chatbot Criticized for Child Safety Failures and Harmful Content
incidents.article_text: Common Sense Media's report finds xAI's Grok chatbot exposes minors to sexual, violent, and inappropriate content, fails to identify underage users, and enables the spread of nonconsensual explicit AI-generated images. These safety failures have led to direct harm to children and teens on the X platform.

event.description: xAI's Grok exposes minors to sexual/violent content, fails to identify underage users

incidents.title: TikTok's AI Moderation Sparks Censorship and Mental Health Concerns in the US
incidents.article_text: Following TikTok's US ownership shift, its AI-driven recommendation and moderation systems have been accused of selectively censoring political content, especially criticism of ICE and Trump, and causing mental health harms among minors. These actions have led to user rights violations, community harm, and widespread user backlash in the United States.

event.description: TikTok AI moderation accused of censoring political content and harming mental health

incidents.title: US Cybersecurity Chief Exposes Sensitive Files via Public ChatGPT
incidents.article_text: Madhu Gottumukkala, acting director of the US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), uploaded sensitive, non-classified government contracting documents to public ChatGPT in August 2025. The incident triggered automated security alerts and an internal review, raising concerns over potential exposure of sensitive information and violation of security protocols.

event.description: US Cybersecurity chief uploaded sensitive government documents to public ChatGPT

incidents.title: Anthropic Secretly Scanned Millions of Books for AI Training, Leading to Copyright Settlement
incidents.article_text: AI start-up Anthropic secretly acquired and destructively scanned millions of books to train its AI models, including the chatbot Claude. This unauthorized use of copyrighted works led to a major lawsuit by authors, resulting in Anthropic agreeing to a $1.5 billion settlement for copyright infringement.

event.description: Anthropic secretly scanned millions of books for AI training, settled for $1.5B

incidents.title: AI-Driven Cyber Attacks Surge Globally, Increasing Harm to Organizations
incidents.article_text: In 2025, organizations worldwide faced a record 1,968 cyber attacks per week—a 70% increase since 2023—driven by attackers leveraging AI and automation. AI has enabled more scalable, personalized, and coordinated attacks, resulting in widespread operational disruption and harm to organizations across multiple sectors.

event.description: AI-driven cyber attacks surged 70% since 2023, 1,968 attacks per week

incidents.title: ChatGPT Health Delivers Inaccurate and Alarmist Health Assessments from Apple Health Data
incidents.article_text: OpenAI's ChatGPT Health, designed to analyze personal health data from apps like Apple Health, has produced inconsistent and alarmist health assessments, as revealed by tests from The Washington Post. Medical experts found these AI-generated diagnoses to be unfounded, raising concerns about user confusion, anxiety, and potential health risks from misleading outputs.

event.description: ChatGPT Health produced inconsistent and alarmist health assessments

incidents.title: TikTok and Meta AI Moderation Systems Accused of Censorship in the US
incidents.article_text: TikTok and Meta's AI-driven content moderation systems have been accused of censoring posts and videos critical of Donald Trump, US immigration enforcement (ICE), and references to Jeffrey Epstein. Users and public figures report blocked content, prompting investigations into potential violations of free expression rights in the US.

event.description: TikTok and Meta AI moderation accused of censoring political criticism

ai_system.name: TikTok and Meta content moderation AI
